reasoning: Massive anti-government protests occurred in Turkey beginning on March 19, 2025, sparked by the arrest of Istanbul Mayor Ekrem İmamoğlu. These protests saw around 300,000 participants in Istanbul and spread to over 50 provinces, making it the largest protest wave in a decade. This event clearly exceeds the 10,000+ participant threshold and definitively qualifies as a significant anti-government protest, which is the exact type of event the Carnegie Endowment's Global Protest Tracker records. Even if the question launched after mid-March (which would exclude the initial wave due to the 'start date after launch' caveat), May Day (May 1) 2025 also saw protests in Istanbul and across Türkiye with reported attendance in the 'tens of thousands', which would serve as an independent trigger. The primary remaining uncertainty is whether the tracker might experience an update hiatus or describe the crowd size vaguely (e.g., 'thousands' instead of explicitly confirming 10k+, though 'hundreds of thousands' is widely reported and likely to be noted). Given the enormous scale and international coverage of the 2025 protests, it is highly probable (0.85) that the tracker will report an event satisfying the criteria.
